decks.notes: The deck focuses on using control elements to disrupt the opponent's strategy while gradually building up resources and value over time. It utilizes a mix of removal spells, hand disruption, and versatile creatures to control the game. The inclusion of powerful cards like Sheoldred, the Apocalypse and Professor Dellian Fel allows the deck to maintain a strong board presence and grind out victories. The mana base is carefully constructed to support the deck's strategy and ensure consistent access to the necessary colors of mana.

This Selesnya Aggro deck focuses on using low-cost creatures like Llanowar Elves and Elvish Mystic to ramp up quickly and overwhelm the opponent with a swarm of creatures. Cards like Thalia, Guardian of Thraben and Archon of Emeria help to disrupt the opponent's game plan, while Skyclave Apparition and Collected Company provide additional removal and creature options. The deck also includes some high-impact finishers like Elspeth, Storm Slayer and The Wandering Emperor to close out games quickly. With a strong mana base and a mix of aggressive and disruptive elements, this deck aims to win quickly and decisively.

This deck focuses on utilizing graveyard strategies and control elements to disrupt opponents and gain advantage. It includes cards that interact with the opponent's hand like Thoughtseize and Duress, removal spells such as Fatal Push and Abrupt Decay, and powerful threats like Sheoldred, the Apocalypse. The mana base is designed to support both black and green spells, with utility lands like Boseiju, Who Endures and Urborg, Tomb of Yawgmoth. Overall, the deck aims to grind out a win by outvaluing the opponent and controlling the game.

The main concept of this Pioneer 1 vs 1 deck is to control the game using a combination of removal spells and powerful creatures. The deck focuses on using cards like Fatal Push, Abrupt Decay, and Witherbloom Charm to deal with opponent's threats, while deploying creatures like Sheoldred, the Apocalypse and Sentinel of the Nameless City to establish board presence. The deck also utilizes cards like Thoughtseize to disrupt opponent's game plan and maintain control throughout the game. Overall, the strategy revolves around efficiently managing resources and outvaluing the opponent to secure victory.

This Selesnya Aggro deck is built around creating a fast and aggressive board presence using creatures like Bishop of Wings, Resplendent Angel, and Righteous Valkyrie. The deck also utilizes mana acceleration with Llanowar Elves and Elvish Mystic to quickly bring out powerful angels like Skyclave Apparition and Giada, Font of Hope. With cards like Collected Company and Kayla's Reconstruction to help maintain pressure on the opponent, this deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a swarm of angels while controlling the battlefield with removal spells and lifegain abilities.

The deck focuses on disrupting and controlling the opponent's resources while building up its own board presence with creatures like Badgermole Cub and Graveyard Trespasser. It utilizes a combination of hand disruption spells like Thoughtseize, removal spells like Fatal Push and Abrupt Decay, and powerful threats like Sheoldred, the Apocalypse to dominate the game. The deck also contains mana ramp with Llanowar Elves and versatile lands like Mutavault to ensure that it can cast its spells efficiently. With a mix of disruption, removal, and threats, the deck aims to outlast and outvalue its opponents.

The deck focuses on controlling the game by disrupting the opponent's hand with Thoughtseize and removal spells like Fatal Push and Abrupt Decay. It also ramps up mana with Llanowar Elves and Mutavault to eventually play powerful threats like Sheoldred, the Apocalypse and Sentinel of the Nameless City. The deck can generate extra value with cards like Culling Ritual and Witherbloom Charm, while also utilizing lands like Wastewood Verge and Urborg, Tomb of Yawgmoth for mana fixing and synergy. Overall, the deck aims to outvalue and outlast opponents in a grindy game.

The main concept of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent quickly with aggressive creatures and cheap spells. By playing efficient creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Soul-Scar Mage alongside burn spells like Burst Lightning and Reckless Rage, the deck aims to deal damage quickly and efficiently. The deck also features card draw spells like Sleight of Hand and Stormchaser's Talent to keep the momentum going. With a focus on tempo and aggression, this UR Aggro deck aims to put pressure on the opponent from the start and close out the game before they can stabilize.

The focus of this deck is to quickly play spells and efficiently discard cards to power up Arclight Phoenix and bring it back from the graveyard to deal consistent damage to the opponent. The deck includes a variety of instant and sorcery spells to trigger the Phoenix's ability, as well as cards like Ledger Shredder and Treasure Cruise to ensure a steady flow of spells. The inclusion of Picklock Prankster and Otawara, Soaring City adds extra synergy with the Phoenix strategy, creating a fast-paced and aggressive play style.

The main strategy of this Dimir Control deck revolves around utilizing a variety of removal spells, counterspells, and disruptive cards to control the game and disrupt the opponent's plans. The deck aims to outvalue the opponent with powerful threats like Sheoldred, the Apocalypse, while also generating card advantage with cards like Poet's Quill. The inclusion of Yorion, Sky Nomad as a companion allows for additional value by flickering permanents for extra card advantage. Overall, the deck seeks to grind out the opponent and dominate the late game with its powerful control tools.

This deck focuses on quickly casting spells to enable the Arclight Phoenix to return from the graveyard to the battlefield. By utilizing low-cost spells like Consider, Fiery Impulse, and Treasure Cruise, the deck aims to fill the graveyard and cast Arclight Phoenix multiple times in a single turn. This aggressive strategy puts pressure on the opponent early in the game, making it difficult for them to stabilize and defend against the recurring threats. The deck's synergy between spells and creatures allows for explosive turns and a fast-paced playstyle.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly assemble the Greasefang Parhelion combo, which involves playing the Greasefang, Okiba Boss alongside the Parhelion II card. The deck includes a mix of protection spells, removal, and mana sources to help facilitate the combo. Additionally, there are cards like Thoughtseize to disrupt the opponent's strategy and keep the combo safe. The deck aims to efficiently control the game until it can assemble the combo and overwhelm the opponent with powerful synergies and interactions.

The main concept of this UR Aggro deck is to quickly and aggressively control the board with cheap spells and creatures, utilizing prowess triggers and card draw effects to maintain pressure on the opponent. The deck aims to efficiently burn through the opponent's life total while being able to bounce threats with cards like Boomerang Basics and Academic Dispute. It also includes cards like Cori-Steel Cutter and Soul-Scar Mage to enhance the deck's prowess abilities and spells like Fiery Impulse for additional removal. Overall, the deck focuses on fast, efficient aggression and board control in Pioneer 1 vs 1 matches.

This deck focuses on utilizing cheap and efficient creatures and burn spells to apply constant pressure on the opponent. By playing aggressive creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Emberheart Challenger, and supporting them with burn spells like Fiery Impulse and Reckless Rage, the deck aims to quickly reduce the opponent's life total to zero. The inclusion of cards like Academic Dispute and Stormchaser's Talent provides card advantage and additional damage output. Overall, the deck is designed to overwhelm the opponent with a fast and powerful aggressive strategy.

The key theme of this deck is to quickly play aggressive creatures and spells to deal damage to the opponent while maintaining card advantage. By utilizing cheap creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Soul-Scar Mage, along with efficient burn spells like Fiery Impulse and Reckless Rage, the deck aims to apply pressure early on and maintain momentum throughout the game. The inclusion of card draw and bounce spells like Academic Dispute and Boomerang Basics helps to ensure that the deck can maintain a steady stream of threats and answers to control the board and secure victory.

This deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's game plan with a combination of removal spells like Fatal Push and Abrupt Decay, along with hand disruption cards like Thoughtseize. The deck also utilizes mana acceleration from cards like Llanowar Elves to ramp into powerful threats like Sheoldred, the Apocalypse. Mutavault and Badgermole Cub provide additional threats and flexibility in combat. The control elements of the deck are supported by the land base, which includes utility lands like Boseiju, Who Endures and Urborg, Tomb of Yawgmoth to further enhance the deck's overall strategy.
